memberdef.argsstring: (self, asyncio.StreamReader reader, asyncio.StreamWriter writer, bool is_dialer=True, float timeout=30.0, Optional[Callable[[PingStats], Awaitable[None]]] on_stats_update=None)
memberdef.name: __init__

para.verbatim:
Initialize ping protocol
Args: reader: Stream reader for the connection writer: Stream writer for the connection is_dialer: True if this peer initiated the connection timeout: Default timeout for ping operations (seconds) on_stats_update: Optional callback when stats are updated

Send ping requests and measure RTT
Args: count: Number of ping requests to send interval: Interval between pings in seconds
Returns: Updated PingStats
Raises: RuntimeError: If not the dialing peer asyncio.TimeoutError: If ping times out ConnectionError: If connection fails

Handle incoming ping requests (server side)
This method echoes back received payloads. It runs until the connection is closed or stop() is called.
Raises: RuntimeError: If already running

Send a single ping request
Returns: RTT in seconds
Raises: asyncio.TimeoutError: If response times out ConnectionError: If connection fails

libp2p Ping Protocol Implementation
The ping protocol measures RTT (Round Trip Time) between peers by: 1. Dialing peer sends 32 random bytes 2. Listening peer echoes back the same bytes 3. Dialing peer measures RTT from send to receive
The dialing peer may repeat the process on the same stream.
